note: Estimates range widely. Modern scholarship suggests 200,000–2 million. 10–20 million people were displaced in the largest mass migration in history.

source: Indian Army Historical Archives / Crisis Group
note: Includes Indian Army, Pakistani forces, and tribals. Precise figures disputed; civilian casualties also occurred but not systematically counted.
Both sides dispute each other's figures. Pakistan claims lower Indian casualties; India claims higher Pakistani losses. ~800 tanks destroyed combined. Civilian casualties in border areas unaccounted.

killed: India: 1,426 / Pakistan: ~9,000 (military + civilians in West front)
source: Indian MOD / Pakistani Hamoodur Rahman Commission
note: India suffered 1,426 killed and 4,004 wounded. 93,000 Pakistani soldiers became POWs. Separate from East Pakistan civilian deaths (300,000–3 million contested).

source: Bangladesh Government / Hamoodur Rahman Commission / Multiple scholars
note: Bangladesh's official figure is 3 million. Pakistan's Hamoodur Rahman Commission cited 26,000. Independent scholarship suggests 300,000–500,000 as most credible estimate. UN has not ruled on the figures.
Most deaths (70%+) from extreme cold, avalanches, and altitude sickness rather than combat. Pakistan's 2012 Gayari avalanche killed 140 soldiers in a single event.

India's figure of 527 killed is official. Pakistan never officially disclosed military losses; independent estimates range 350–1,000. Pakistan initially denied NLI involvement, complicating accounting.

source: South Asia Terrorism Portal (SATP) / Indian MHA
note: Includes militants, Indian security forces, and civilians. Kashmiri groups allege higher civilian toll. Most deaths occurred 1989–2002; significant reduction after 2004 but continued incidents.
166 killed in the 60-hour attack across multiple sites. 9 of 10 attackers killed; Ajmal Kasab captured, tried, and executed November 2012.
Suicide bombing by JeM operative Adil Ahmad Dar killed 40 CRPF personnel in the Pulwama attack — the deadliest militant attack in Kashmir since the 1989 insurgency began.
Four JeM militants killed 19 Indian Army personnel in predawn attack on Brigade HQ at Uri, Baramulla. Deadliest attack on Indian Army in 30 years. All attackers killed.
Over 800 Indian soldiers died in accidents, landmine explosions, and disease during the 10-month military standoff — without a single combat engagement.
9 Indian security guards and personnel killed by 5 LeT/JeM gunmen; all attackers killed. Delhi Police identified Pakistani nationals; attack nearly triggered full-scale war.
Militants attacked tourists at Baisaran meadow, Pahalgam on April 22, 2025. The attack triggered India's suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty and Operation Sindoor.

India struck nine Pakistan/AJK militant targets. Pakistan claimed 31 Indian kills in retaliatory fire; India claimed Pakistani military and civilian casualties from Pakistani shelling. Both sides dispute the other's numbers. Ceasefire on May 10.
